summary: Added a GitHub Actions build workflow that builds the repository on push and pull_request using a devkitPPC container, downloads the required MWCC compiler bundle, runs make with GENERATE_MAP=1, and therefore exercises calcprogress in CI. The Makefile was adjusted for Unix CI defaults by silencing Wine debug output, setting a default DEVKITPPC path, and passing explicit DOL and MAP paths to calcprogress.py. calcprogress.py was made more reusable by reading the DOL and map file paths from command-line arguments instead of hardcoded project paths.
what_changed: Created .github/workflows/build.yml with a Build workflow triggered on push and pull_request.
Configured the CI job to run on ubuntu-latest inside the devkitpro/devkitppc:latest container.
Installed build-essential and wine32 in the workflow after enabling i386 architecture.
Downloaded GC_WII_COMPILERS.zip and moved the extracted GC compiler directory to tools/mwcc_compiler.
Ran GENERATE_MAP=1 make -j$(nproc) in CI so the build also emits a map file and invokes progress calculation.
In the Makefile, exported WINEDEBUG ?= -all on non-Windows platforms to suppress noisy Wine debug output.
In the Makefile, set DEVKITPPC ?= /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC on non-Windows platforms as a default that can still be overridden.
Changed the GENERATE_MAP post-build calcprogress invocation from tools/calcprogress.py $@ to tools/calcprogress.py $(DOL) $(MAP).
Removed hardcoded DOL_PATH and MAP_PATH constants from tools/calcprogress.py.
Changed calcprogress.py to open sys.argv[1] for the DOL file and sys.argv[2] for the map file.
smart_moves: The workflow validates both full build correctness and progress-report generation by using GENERATE_MAP=1 rather than only compiling objects.
Making calcprogress.py accept explicit DOL and MAP paths decouples it from one hardcoded build layout and makes it usable from Makefile targets and CI.
Using DEVKITPPC ?= preserves user overrides while giving Unix and container builds a sensible default.
Suppressing Wine debug output with WINEDEBUG ?= -all improves CI log readability without forcing the setting when users explicitly override it.
The workflow installs wine32 and sets WINEPREFIX inside the GitHub workspace, matching the need to run Windows MWCC tools under Wine in Linux CI.
Downloading compilers during CI keeps the repository from vendoring the compiler payload while still making the action self-contained enough to build.
decomp_lessons: CI for decompilation projects should exercise the same map-producing path used for progress accounting, not just object compilation.
Tools used by Makefile targets should accept file paths as arguments instead of assuming one hardcoded baserom or build directory.
When adding Unix/Linux support around Windows compiler tooling, Wine noise can obscure real build failures; set quiet defaults where possible.
Use conditional/default Makefile assignment such as ?= for environment-sensitive paths so local developer setups and CI containers can coexist.
A build workflow can document the expected external toolchain layout, such as tools/mwcc_compiler and /opt/devkitpro/devkitPPC, by encoding it in CI setup.
naming_conventions: GitHub workflow name used plain title case: Build.
Compiler archive contents were normalized from tools/GC to tools/mwcc_compiler, matching the repository's expected MWCC tool directory.
Makefile variables retained uppercase names for build artifacts and toggles: GENERATE_MAP, DEVKITPPC, WINEDEBUG, DOL, MAP.
calcprogress.py switched away from module-level DOL_PATH and MAP_PATH constants once paths became runtime arguments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching changes were made.
The relevant matching-support tactic was infrastructure-oriented: build with GENERATE_MAP=1 so CI produces a map file and calcprogress can measure decompiled code size.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly stated the motivation: build the whole repo and run calcprogress on every commit and pull request.
The PR body noted calcprogress.py was modified to use passed-in DOL and map files instead of hardcoded values.
The PR body noted Unix defaults for DEVKITPPC and silenced Wine debug output.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify .github/workflows/build.yml to update compiler download sources, containers, or dependency installation.
Find later PRs that add argument validation or usage messages to tools/calcprogress.py after it began reading sys.argv[1] and sys.argv[2].
Search for changes to DEVKITPPC or WINEDEBUG defaults in the Makefile to see whether this CI setup required portability fixes.
Search for later CI failures or fixes involving wine32, devkitpro/devkitppc:latest, or MWCC compiler download availability.
